mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 8.0 percent, labor market slack dissipates, triggering wage growth expectations that boost consumer spending and corporate revenue forecasts, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day forward returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE drops below 8.0, or the signal's directional accuracy falls below 52% over a 3-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in housing starts and revolving consumer credit indicate synchronized household balance sheet expansion, which amplifies aggregate demand shocks and lifts equity valuations over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces a negative or zero correlation with 20-day SPX returns across three consecutive rolling 5-year windows, or the interaction term's coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A rising industrial production index signals capacity utilization recovery and supply chain normalization, which expands corporate profit margins and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: 60-day forward SPX returns remain flat or negative when INDPRO rises for three consecutive months, or the signal's information coefficient drops below 0.02 in a walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: When real GDP growth exceeds 2.0 percent annualized, corporate earnings growth accelerates beyond inflation expectations, providing fundamental support that pushes S&P 500 prices higher over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 60% of quarters where GDPC1 growth surpasses the 0.5 percent threshold, or the signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark over a 5-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Steady month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects persistent labor demand, which sustains consumer income streams and supports equity cash flow projections, yielding positive S&P 500 returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: The rolling 12-month correlation between monthly PAYEMS changes and 5-day SPX returns falls below 0.10, or forward returns show no statistically significant difference between positive and negative payroll growth months.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of rising consumer price inflation and stable federal funds rates compresses real interest rates, reducing the cost of capital for leveraged firms and driving equity multiples higher over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The interaction term between CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS yields a negative coefficient in a multivariate regression of 60-day SPX returns, or the signal's hit rate drops below 48% over a 3-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in revolving consumer credit indicates improving household liquidity and risk appetite, which directly fuels discretionary spending and lifts S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 55% of months where RSAFS exceeds 320000.0, or the signal's predictive power vanishes when controlling for FEDFUNDS in a multivariate model.
